model.label: GPT-5.4
model.description: OpenAI • Frontier GPT model for general-purpose reasoning, tool use, and agentic workflows.

pick.rationale: Duke is the pick because the full-season quality gap is still enormous: the Blue Devils are No. 1 in both KenPom and Torvik with elite top-five offense and the nation’s best defense, while TCU profiles more like a solid top-50 team built on defense but with a much less efficient offense. TCU does have a credible upset path if it turns this into a physical, lower-scoring game and Duke’s health issues materially shorten the rotation, but the available reporting did not surface a confirmed absence big enough to outweigh Duke’s two-way dominance. In a Round of 32 game, Duke remains the much safer advance choice.

reasoningStep.title: Duke’s two-way edge outweighs TCU’s upset path
reasoningStep.summary: I checked predictive ratings and recent availability context, then weighed whether TCU’s defense created a realistic upset path. Duke’s overall efficiency edge is so large that the Blue Devils are still the clear and safer bracket pick.
evidence: KenPom: Duke No. 1 (AdjEM 39.17, AdjO 127.9 No. 4, AdjD 88.7) vs TCU No. 43 (AdjEM 17.62, AdjO 115.5 No. 81, AdjD 97.8).
Torvik: Duke No. 1 (AdjO 128.0 No. 4, AdjD 90.45 No. 1) vs TCU No. 48 (AdjO 114.86 No. 83, AdjD 99.63 No. 30).
Recent news/web checks surfaced Duke injury discussion but no clearly confirmed absence significant enough to change the pick.
TCU’s best path is a defensive, lower-possession game, but its offense rates far below Duke’s and leaves little margin for an upset.

toolCalls.summary: Returned combined Torvik/KenPom ratings for 2 teams.

Scheyer: Foster had right-foot surgery; unlikely to play before Final Four. Ngongba improving with therapy but remains day-by-day, no firm timeline. Injuries could affect NCAA seeding; Boozer had back-to-back 16-point games. Charlotte Caleb Foster and Patrick Ngongba watched from the bench in matching black hoodies and boots on their right feet as No. 1 seed Duke defeated No. 2 seed Virginia, 74-70, to win the ACC Championship on Saturday night at Charlotte’s Spectrum Center. After the game, Duke coach Jon Scheyer offered an update on both players as the No. 1 Blue Devils turn their attention to the NCAA Tournament. modelTrace.content:
You are an analyst predicting a single March Madness game inside a bracket run.
You have live tools. Use them before making a pick when they can reduce uncertainty.
You are not filling the whole bracket in one response. Predict only the current game.
Use prior picks as already committed bracket state.
You may use at most 10 tool rounds before you must finalize your answer.
Before making a pick, investigate relevant context such as injuries or availability concerns, recent team news, prior head-to-head results when useful, and multiple raw statistics from the available ratings and matchup data.
Take a holistic approach. Balance team quality, matchup specifics, health, form, schedule context, and upset risk instead of relying on one metric.
Do not force an upset, but explicitly consider whether the underdog has a credible path to win and let that affect confidence and rationale.
Return strict JSON only.
Final JSON keys: pick, reasoningStep.
pick must contain gameId, winnerId, confidence, rationale.
The rationale must be a short paragraph of 2 to 4 sentences explaining why the winner was chosen.
reasoningStep must contain id, title, summary, evidence.

Tournament year: 2026
Current game id: r32-east-1
Current game label: East Round of 32 1
Round: Round of 32
Resolved teams: Duke (duke) vs TCU (tcu)
Prior committed picks: 36
